transcript: hey everybody Claire here and today I am so excited to share this recipe with you it's actually inspired by one of the sorbets I had on my honeymoon in Italy it was the darkest dark chocolate sorbet it was black it was absolutely gorgeous but it had a really delicious very pure chocolate flavor and I was obsessed with it and I've been wanting to make it ever since. And so today I'm partnering with Chateau Saint Michel and I thought this would be an amazing pairing with the dark chocolate sorbet. So their Merlot has a little bit of a chocolate note to it so it actually is going to be in the sorbet. So this is going to be very elegant, very sophisticated and absolutely delicious. So if you love chocolate, which I'm assuming everybody out there loves chocolate, so this will be for you. So to make it, I'm going to use one cup of my Chateau Saint-Michel Merlot. I'm going to have about a cup and a quarter of water, one cup of sugar, a cup and a half of unsweetened cocoa powder, and I know it sounds silly, but use the best you can find because this, along with the the wine is a really core part of the flavor profile here. I then have two ounces of chocolate ready to melt. I have one teaspoon of vanilla. I have a quarter teaspoon of salt. It's going to be super delicious and make sure to stick around until the end of the video because Chateau Saint-Michel has a really awesome competition going on that you are going really wanna hear about. So to make the sorbet, I am just gonna combine everything in the pot. So I'm gonna add my water, the sugar, the cocoa powder, the vanilla, and the salt. I'm going to hold off on adding the chocolate until the very end because I just want this to melt together, so that'll happen later. All right, and so now I'm gonna add about a cup of wine. And the cool thing is if you really wanna add more, you totally can. If you wanna add less, you can add less. So the sorbet base has fully come together, so now I'm just gonna add the chocolate to melt. And I'm just gonna stir it, and it should start melting immediately. And this is a place where you can sort of go as sweet or not sweet as you want. I'm doing semi-sweet because I think it's really balanced but if you wanted to do milk chocolate or super dark chocolate you could do that as well. So now I'm just gonna pour this off and then chill it in the fridge and then once it's ready to go I will pop it into my ice cream maker. So my sorbet base is out of the fridge it is perfectly. And this is my ice cream maker. You can use whatever ice cream maker you want, but here's the thing. You just want to make sure that this tub part is super duper cold. It has to be, I put it in the freezer overnight. It definitely helps if this is super, super cold. All right. So I'm just going to pour this in and then get mixing. I'm gonna let this go for, I'd say, about 20 minutes. What I'm looking for is for the sorbet to have the consistency of soft serve. So you actually want it to be really fully formed. As a chocolate lover, that is everything. Oh my gosh, it's so, so rich. What I love about it, too, is the wine gives this really amazing back note, but it's not like a wine sorbet, which would also be awesome, but it's not necessarily that. The chocolate is first and foremost, but the Merlot really gives it this amazing depth of flavor. So this is an awesome combination. I really love it.
